mechanism_narrative: A higher 3-month growth rate in PAYEMS signals expanding labor income and lower near-term recession risk, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS 3-month growth has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window or top-growth observations do not outperform bottom-growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.5%, restrictive policy raises equity discount rates and tightens credit conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or have mean returns no lower than untriggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month RSAFS growth combined with a decelerating CPIAUCSL trend signals real consumption resilience without renewed inflation pressure, causing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate below its prior 3-month rate
failure_condition: Reject if the joint signal has no positive return spread versus periods with weak RSAFS or accelerating CPIAUCSL, or if 60-day forward hit rate is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in INDPRO greater than 0.5% over three months signals weakening goods-sector output and margin pressure, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 0.5% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO declines are followed by nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the negative-return hit rate is not above 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A UNRATE increase greater than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor-market deterioration and rising recession probability, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or show no downside skew versus baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST crosses above 1300 and rises over three months, housing activity adds to construction demand and household durables spending, causing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses above 1300 and has positive 3-month change
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakout periods do not deliver higher mean 60-day S&P 500 returns than non-breakout periods or if the signal Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS above 3.5% together with a rising UNRATE signals a policy-error regime in which restrictive rates meet weakening labor demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 3.5% and UNRATE rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS and UNRATE trigger fails to produce lower 5-day returns than either condition alone or if triggered mean returns are positive.

mechanism_narrative: Positive GDPC1 growth paired with negative 3-month INDPRO growth signals a growth divergence where headline demand lags real-time production weakness, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the GDPC1-INDPRO divergence has no negative 60-day return spread versus aligned growth periods or if walk-forward Rank IC is greater than or equal to zero.
